summary: Backend-only testing of Niva Novus API. 32/33 tests passed (97%). All auth (OTP+email), RBAC, devices, rooms, scenes, jobs, tickets, leads, customers, inventory, invoices, AMC, analytics, energy, chat and Stripe checkout session creation work correctly. Only failure: GET /api/payments/checkout/status/{session_id} returns 500 because Stripe rejects retrieval of the freshly created session ID (likely an Emergent test-wrapper limitation), and the endpoint lacks try/except.

critical.issue: Returns 500 InternalServerError. Stripe raises 'No such checkout.session' for sessions created via STRIPE_API_KEY=sk_test_emergent. CheckoutError exception is not handled in server.py:518. Should be wrapped in try/except and return 4xx with payment_status from local payment_transactions doc as fallback.

action_items: Wrap StripeCheckout.get_checkout_status() call in try/except in /api/payments/checkout/status endpoint. On CheckoutError, fall back to reading local payment_transactions collection by session_id and return its status/payment_status fields (HTTP 200) instead of raising 500. Also log the underlying error.
Verify whether sk_test_emergent (Emergent test wrapper) actually supports session retrieval. If not, the local DB fallback is mandatory for the billing return-flow to work.
critical_code_review_comments: server.py:259 - The list comprehension `[p['id'] async for p in db.projects.find(...)]` inside an inline dict for non-admin users runs even for admin (short-circuited by ternary, but readability is poor). Consider extracting to helper.
server.py:289 update_job has no role guard - any authenticated user (including customer) can PATCH any job. Should be restricted to technician/admin or scoped by ownership.
server.py:316 update_ticket has no role guard - customers can call PATCH /api/tickets/{tid} for tickets that are not theirs. Should restrict to admin or owner.
server.py:283 GET /api/jobs/{id} has no scoping - any authenticated user can fetch any job by id (information leak).
server.py:258 energy_summary - power consumption calculation uses 0.012 magic constant; document units (W * 0.012 -> kWh assumption is unclear).

server.py:543 stripe_webhook - logger is referenced before being initialized (line 583). NameError will occur if exception path hits before logger assignment at module load (works because it's lazy at call time, but fragile).
server.py:518 - Missing try/except around external Stripe API call (root cause of failing test).
JWT_SECRET fallback default 'niva-secret' in code; .env has explicit value but defensive default in code reduces secret hygiene.
seed_data_creation: Used existing seeded data (auto-seed on startup). Test created TEST_-prefixed tickets and chat messages; not cleaned up.
context_for_next_testing_agent: Backend is mostly green. Re-run /app/backend/tests/backend_test.py after main agent fixes /api/payments/checkout/status (add try/except + DB fallback). Note: test_checkout_session and test_create_ticket emit a benign PytestReturnNotNoneWarning (return value used by other tests) - not a failure.
rca_of_the_issue: Root cause: server.py:514-518 directly awaits sc.get_checkout_status(session_id) with no exception handling. emergentintegrations.payments.stripe.checkout.get_checkout_status calls stripe.checkout.Session.retrieve which raises stripe._error.InvalidRequestError ('No such checkout.session: cs_test_...') when querying a session that was created via the Emergent test key wrapper. The library re-raises as CheckoutError, which propagates to FastAPI as a 500. Reproduction: POST /api/payments/checkout/session with package_id=amc_premium -> get session_id -> immediately GET /api/payments/checkout/status/{session_id} -> 500. Mitigation: try/except CheckoutError, return cached payment_transactions doc data from MongoDB instead.
